title: Ridely: Building the Future of Smart Deliveries in Africa
excerpt: Ridely is a smart dispatch and delivery platform designed to simplify logistics in Africa through real-time tracking, pricing transparency, and secure deliveries.
author: Trust Williams
content:
# Ridely

Ridely is a smart delivery and dispatch platform designed to simplify how packages are sent and received. Built to reduce risk, increase transparency, and provide real-time tracking, Ridely solves key logistics challenges in African cities.

## Problem Statement
- Unreliable delivery systems
- No transparency on rider location
- Poor user experience
- High risk of loss
- Manual coordination

## Solution
Ridely enables users to:
- Request a rider
- Enter pickup + delivery address
- See live rider tracking
- Get real-time cost estimation based on distance and parcel size
- Fully track delivery progress

## Key Features
- Real-time GPS tracking
- Distance-based pricing
- Parcel size selection
- Secure rider system
- In-app communication
- Delivery history
- Ratings and trust system

## Tech Stack / Implementation Idea
- React Native for user & rider apps
- Next.js for website
- Node.js backend
- Map integration
- Cloud database
- WebSockets for live tracking

## Use case
"I need to send a package urgently and want to track it in real time."

## Future Vision
Ridely evolving into:
- Package + food + document delivery
- Cross-city logistics
- Business dashboards
- Integration with e-commerce

## CTA
Join Ridely's journey into the future of African deliveries.

title: CryptoSplit: Splitting Bills in the Web3 Era Using Smart Contracts
excerpt: CryptoSplit is a decentralized way to split bills and manage group payments using secure smart contracts.
author: Trust Williams
content:
# CryptoSplit

CryptoSplit is a concept designed to solve one of Web3’s everyday problems: splitting expenses safely and transparently using smart contracts.

## What is CryptoSplit?
CryptoSplit allows:
- Group expenses to be created
- Wallets to connect
- Smart contracts to enforce split
- Participants to pay their portion
- Automated release when fully funded

## Why it matters
Traditional apps require trust. CryptoSplit uses:
- Code-based trust
- Transparent ledger
- Automated execution
- No middleman

## Features
- Wallet connect
- Smart contract creation
- Auto split calculation
- Payment deadlines
- Refund trigger if unpaid
- DAO group mode

## Example
"A group of friends splits a hotel bill using CryptoSplit in minutes."

## Business & Startup Potential
- Web3 families & teams
- Freelancer billing
- Community funding
- Event cost sharing

## Future Vision
CryptoSplit could become:
- A Web3 payment layer
- A global micro-transaction network
- A DAO expense manager

## CTA
CryptoSplit isn’t just an app — it’s the future of shared value.

title: TOON vs JSON: Why Token Oriented Object Notation is the Future of Data
excerpt: A deep technical breakdown of why Token Oriented Object Notation (TOON) is more powerful, secure, and efficient than JSON in token-based systems.
author: Trust Williams
content:
# TOON vs JSON

TOON (Token Oriented Object Notation) is a data format designed specifically for token-based ecosystems, making it ideal for Web3, smart contracts, and decentralized systems.

## Difference from JSON
JSON is:
- Text-based
- General purpose
- No built-in token logic
- Requires heavy parsing

TOON is:
- Token-first
- Lightweight
- Structured for decentralized validation
- Optimized for blockchain storage
- Faster in token-based systems

## Sample JSON
```
{
  "user": {
    "id": "123",
    "name": "Alice",
    "balance": 1000
  }
}
```,

## Sample TOON
```
token user {
  id: 123;
  name: Alice;
  balance: 1000;
}
```,

## Advantages of TOON
- Built-in token semantics
- Reduced parsing overhead
- Enhanced security for token data
- Better suited for smart contracts
- More efficient storage on-chain

## Use Cases
- Decentralized applications (dApps)
- Smart contract data exchange
- Token management systems
- Blockchain-based identity solutions

## Future Vision
TOON could become the standard for data exchange in tokenized ecosystems, driving efficiency and security in Web3 applications.

## CTA
Developers and organizations should consider adopting TOON for their token-based projects to leverage its unique advantages over JSON.
